ab: maki te sa’ing seng pusan sa’ing kawas ga, sexu pklahang ku splawa naha lnhoyan na pkkayan yaw nku lalu mibunsyo tayan ga ana mosa sbiru lalu tayan nanak.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: nwahan mu mita Nolay Piho mlikuy na alang tayan, heya ga bsya calay mskura nya sexu pklahang ru msina sku mibunsyo ta ga aki nanak lalu tayan sbiru ta.
ch: 我訪問了的部落青年Nolay Piho，他長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」。
en: I interviewed Nolay Piho, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: pkayan cami ’nyan hngawan ryax hani ga mosa cu mita sali nya.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: maki sku pkayan myan ga, sba’an cu nya kahun inu lalu nya, spkita cu nya utux sali nya ru alang nya uyi.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: kyalun mu heya maha ca utux ci lungan su mosa p’iyu lalu, ya’eh su pbiru na lela heya ga?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: kyalun cu nya maha, baha mcwa sbiru ta ke ckoku ga ini ba’i kmayan lalu nya, kruma c’oli ga No先生 san nya, piyux calay mhtu psya’an na ke.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「No先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"No Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: kmayan Nolay Piho maha, “sbiru ta ke Tayal lalu ta ga yaca ga calay ci lalu ta, lalu ta ga tgiwan na gluw ta Tayal, gluw utux sali, ru cnrxan ta, aki piyux hayi gluw tayan ga mosa naha llwan lalu naha nanak.”
ch: Nolay Piho說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Nolay Piho said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
